sourceMovie.title: The Secret Agent
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Secret Agent works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Perfect World
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Secret Agent who respond to its crime will find A Perfect World (1993) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Purple Noon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Secret Agent fans keep landing on Purple Noon (1960) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Heaven draws from the same crime well as The Secret Agent, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2002 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Repo Men
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Repo Men (2010) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that The Secret Agent later expanded. If The Secret Agent resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: The Dead Pool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of The Secret Agent — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into The Dead Pool (1988). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Point of No Return
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Point of No Return (1993) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps The Secret Agent rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Beirut
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Beirut (2018) is the kind of film The Secret Agent fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Nighthawks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nighthawks shares The Secret Agent's gift for crime — the 1981 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Missing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Secret Agent compelling — the drama, the thriller — shows up in Missing (1982) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: 6 Bullets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of The Secret Agent is alive in 6 Bullets (2012), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Secret Agent?
faq.answer: Try A Perfect World, Purple Noon, Heaven—each shares DNA with The Secret Agent while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Secret Agent?
faq.answer: The Secret Agent (2025) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Secret Agent worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
